findings.description: The SQL code example in s4 omits two elements from the source: (1) the Nablarch SQL statement identifier `SELECT_ACTIVE_APPLICATION_BY_CONDITION=` that precedes the WITH clause, and (2) the `ORDER BY WORK_ITEM.APPLICATION_DATE` clause at the end of the query. The statement identifier is how Java code references this SQL via `getParameterizedSqlStatement()`.
findings.source_evidence: Source SQL begins with `SELECT_ACTIVE_APPLICATION_BY_CONDITION=\nWITH` and ends with `ORDER BY\n    WORK_ITEM.APPLICATION_DATE`

findings.description: `ThreadContext` is a PascalCase class name used in the s1 code example (`ThreadContext.getUserId()`) but is absent from the s1 hints array.

Three identifiers from the s5 source code are missing from hints: `@OnError` and `@OnErrors` (annotation names on the example action methods) and `ExclusiveCtrlTransExpeApplicationContext` (PascalCase class name passed to `prepareVersion`).
findings.source_evidence: Source s5 code uses `@OnError(type = ApplicationException.class, ...)`, `@OnErrors({ @OnError(type = OptimisticLockException.class, ...) })`, and `new ExclusiveCtrlTransExpeApplicationContext(applicationId)`
